content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 17, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 17 focused on stabilizing the elizaOS Live USB architecture, hardening the provisioning-worker daemon, and refining cross-platform mobile/desktop integration. Significant progress was made in resolving local-inference startup issues and consolidating CI workflows, alongside successful hardware validation for speculative decoding on Intel Lunar Lake platforms.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**elizaOS Live USB & OS Infrastructure**
*   Advanced the elizaOS Live USB variant with production-ready update architecture, including signed manifests and systemd-based health checks ([#7762](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7762)).
*   Hardened integration for the milady-tails variant, ensuring correct branding, root-mode boot fixes, and UDisks typelib support ([#7757](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7757), [#7776](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7776)).

**Provisioning & Cloud Orchestration**
*   Folded node health checks directly into the `provisioning-worker` daemon to eliminate reliance on the legacy `container-control-plane` service ([#7775](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7775)).
*   Optimized cron path handling by moving SSH-bound work directly to the provisioning-worker ([#7778](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7778)).
*   Consolidated Docker image build workflows to resolve manifest errors during CI ([#7780](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7780), [#7777](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7777)).

**Desktop & Mobile Stability**
*   Stabilized Linux local-source startup and chat delivery by improving Electrobun boot progress tracking ([#7781](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7781), [#7773](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7773)).
*   Refined Android native integration, including single-launcher icon enforcement, speculative decoding shim management, and native TTS playback via TalkMode ([#7772](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7772), [#7771](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7771), [#7763](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7763)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#7782](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7782): Connector capability detection, orchestrator hardening, and sub-agent UX redesign.
*   [#7779](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7779): [WIP] First-party Satellites and dynamic view host bridge.
*   [#7774](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7774): Shell foundation v1 and onboarding accessibility/contrast pass.
*   [#7766](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7766): Swift runtime RPC bridge.

## CLOSED ISSUES

**Speculative Decoding & Hardware Validation**
*   Validated M-RoPE compatibility for Qwen3.5 speculative decoding on Intel Lunar Lake/Vulkan hardware, confirming stable performance ([#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631)).
*   Resolved the Qwen2.5-14B bandwidth-efficiency cliff on Lunar Lake iGPUs, identifying it as a load-dependent thermal/workload artifact rather than a persistent kernel-selection bug ([#7638](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7638)).

**Community Integration**
*   Closed the SunfishLoop integration idea after initial discussion regarding cross-agent social networking ([#7758](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7758)).

## NEW ISSUES
*   [#7770](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7770): Configuration of release credentials for the cross-platform OTA pipeline.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

**OpenVINO Runtime Kernel ([#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633))**
*   Hardware probing on Intel Lunar Lake confirms the system correctly detects GPU/NPU nodes but lacks the OpenVINO runtime environment. Discussions emphasize that OpenVINO should be scoped to ASR (Whisper) workloads rather than autoregressive LLM generation, as NPU performance for chat-tg is currently suboptimal compared to Vulkan/Metal paths.

**Speculative Decoding SWA Gate ([#7635](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7635))**
*   Validation confirms that the SWA-aware probe-fallback patch successfully enables speculative decoding for SWA-based models. The fix is verified via build-path inclusion and runtime logs, though full end-to-end validation on 27B+ models remains pending due to VRAM constraints on test hardware.
